key_themes: Strategic specialty acquisitions (e.g. $1.1B EyeSouth retina deal)
Strong analyst consensus (Strong Buy ratings, avg PT ~$403)
Resilient scale, contract depth, and steady earnings power in pharma distribution
Upcoming Q2 2026 earnings with expected 8.8% EPS growth
Recent share price pullback viewed as buying opportunity
summary: Cencora (COR), the pharmaceutical distribution and specialty services giant, is generating positive sentiment driven by its $1.1 billion EyeSouth retina acquisition to expand high-margin specialty offerings alongside earlier OneOncology moves and animal health divestiture for focus.[[1]](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/COR.N/)[[2]](https://public.com/stocks/cor/news) Analysts overwhelmingly rate it Strong Buy with an average price target near $403 (vs. recent price ~$328), citing consistent earnings beats, raised guidance, and long-term growth in oncology/retina.[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/COR/)[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/COR/) Despite a double-digit recent pullback and some insider selling, investor discussions frame it as a stable, undervalued healthcare leader with solid upside ahead of May 6 Q2 earnings.[[4]](https://seekingalpha.com/article/4890045-all-eyes-on-cencora-a-healthcare-supplier-that-could-see-lots-more-upside)
sources: 20+ news articles (Yahoo Finance, Reuters, TipRanks, CNBC, Seeking Alpha), 8+ relevant X posts (low overall volume), Multiple Seeking Alpha analyst reports and discussions
